items.title: Nuclear Power Is Still Incredibly Inefficient - Adam Brown
Can LLMs make significant new discoveries?
Why Companies Are Quietly Rehiring Software Engineers...
items.description:
Is the rumour true that companies fired their developers, replaced them with AI, and are now quietly hiring them back? In this episode of Modern Software Engineering, Dave Farley takes a look at the data to bust the myths surrounding tech layoffs, AI vs developer productivity, and what is really happening in the software development job market.

While headlines claim AI vs software engineering is a done deal, actual controlled experiments reveal a massive gap between perception and reality. Discover why developers were measurably 19% slower when coding with AI assistants, and why relying on automated tools without human expertise can increase critical vulnerabilities by up to 40%.

We dive deep into the crucial difference between programming and software engineering. 

Key Topics Covered:
- The truth behind the ai layoffs and rehiring headlines.
- Data from real randomised controlled trials on coding with ai.
- Why the market is repricing pure programming but paying more for software engineering.
- The technical debt trap: how code duplication and security risks accumulate with AI.

-

🎓 OUR GUIDE TO GETTING YOUR NEXT JOB:

Advice and tips for you to think about: the type of organisation that might be the best fit for you, the questions YOU might want to ask at interview, and, possible ‘red flags’ that mean you should probably give that job or employer a wide berth!

Get your guide HERE ➡️ https://www.subscribepage.com/get-your-ideal-sw-dev-job

items.title: The Forgotten Apple Product that Revolutionized Computing #apple #computer
items.description:
The Apple II was Apple’s first commercially successful computer, released in 1977 and selling nearly 6 million units. Starting with just 4KB of RAM and powered by the MOS 6502 CPU, this early personal computer could run applications and classic games like The Oregon Trail. In this teardown, we explore the Apple II’s internal hardware, including ROM, DRAM, and expansion slots, and break down how this revolutionary system helped shape modern computing. From its $7,000 starting price to its simple yet powerful architecture, the Apple II is a key piece of tech history.


Watch the full breakdown:
https://youtu.be/16zrEPOsIcI

#Shorts #AppleII #AppleComputer #RetroTech #VintageComputers #ComputerHistory #RetroComputing #ClassicTech #TechHistory #HowComputersWork

items.title: You need to read less code (hear me out)
items.description:
I once generated 10,000 lines of JavaScript just to organize 100 files without reading any of it, and it worked perfectly. That ratio is exactly why the engineers who refuse to let AI touch their "important" code are falling behind. You should be using AI-generated code from Claude Code, Codex, and Grok to build test harnesses, custom debuggers, and verification systems around the code that actually matters because you're not writing slop, you're verifying your code with even more code. 

Thank you WorkOS for sponsoring! Check them out at: https://soydev.link/workos

items.title: The Artillery Officer Who Solved Einstein's Equations - Adam Brown
A CPU Made of Atoms: IBM's Breakthrough 0.7nm Transistors
items.description:
IBM reckons Moore's Law isn't finished, and their new 7A Nanostack is how they're proving it. Ask Nvidia and they'll tell you scaling is dead. Ask TSMC and they don't seem to care either way. IBM lands firmly on yes, scaling still works.

In this video I go through what 7A Nanostack is and how it works. We start with the basics of a transistor, cover the shift from planar to FinFET to gate-all-around, then get to how IBM stacks an NMOS and a PMOS transistor on top of each other in what the industry calls a complementary FET, or CFET.

[00:00] Moore's Law: alive or dead?
[00:30] What 7A means
[01:56] Planar, FinFET, gate-all-around
[03:06] IBM's 2021 gate-all-around
[06:03] CFET explained
[08:15] Monolithic vs sequential CFET
[12:55] IBM's staggered design
[14:20] The performance numbers
[17:22] The secret bonding oxide

Papers:
Reboh et al, NanoStack Transistor Architecture for CMOS 7A Node and Beyond, VLSI 2025: https://dx.doi.org/10.23919/VLSITechnologyandCir65189.2025.11074866
Zhang et al, Area and Performance of Staggered-Channel Nanostack SRAM Bitcells, VLSI 2026: https://dx.doi.org/10.1109/VLSITechnologyandCir65830.2026.11577402 

Substack: https://morethanmoore.substack.com/p/ibms-announces-07nm-process-node

IBM announcement: https://newsroom.ibm.com/2026-06-25-ibm-debuts-worlds-first-sub-1-nanometer-chip-technology

There's Plenty Moore Room: IBM's New 2nm CPU: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DZ0yfEnwipo
Monolithic 3D: Stacking Without Chiplets: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PVyz-r9wlQo
We Know How to Get Beyond 0.2 Nanometers: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0wRvbIaTUQw
The Truth Behind Huawei's 1.4nm LogicFolding Claim: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=N9aSPnbz3co
TSMC Technology Interview: A16 Node, System-on-Wafer, and High-NA: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WgJnzygxBk0
This 2nm wafer costs HOW MUCH?: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PpBagorVtC0

-----------------------

items.title: WebAssembly on Kubernetes • Nicolas Frankel • YOW! 2025
items.description:
This presentation was recorded at YOW! Australia 2025. #GOTOcon #YOW
https://yowcon.com

Nicolas Fränkel - Developer & Architect @NicolasFrankel 

RESOURCES
https://bsky.app/profile/frankel.ch
https://mastodon.top/@frankel
https://twitter.com/nicolas_frankel
https://github.com/nfrankel
https://www.linkedin.com/in/nicolasfrankel
https://blog.frankel.ch

Links
https://kubernetes.o
https://webassembly.org
https://youtu.be/yabE04SD5bQ
https://wasmer.io/posts/python-on-the-edge-powered-by-webassembly
https://www.fermyon.com/blog/how-to-think-about-wasm
https://wasmedge.org/docs/develop/deploy/intro

ABSTRACT
WebAssembly started as a technology tailored to web browsers and is becoming popular as a server-side technology as well. The next step is for Wasm to become a powerful tool for cloud-native applications. When combined with Kubernetes, WebAssembly can revolutionize application deployment, security, and resource efficiency in ways traditional containers cannot.

This talk explores why and how to leverage WebAssembly within Kubernetes environments to create scalable, high-performance, and secure applications. [...]

TIMECODES
00:00 Intro
02:14 WebAssembly in 2018
05:00 WebAssembly in 2020
07:30 WebAssembly now
10:05 The many moving facets of WASM on k8s
11:00 The demo points today
14:20 Demo
24:07 Just enough k8s intrinsics
25:44 WASMedge
26:11 Demo
33:32 The good, the bad & the ugly
35:44 Summary
36:21 Outro

Download slides and read the full abstract here:
https://yowcon.com/brisbane-2025/sessions/3619
